Meeting Public Sector Requirements Made Simple

We understand the unique challenges of public procurement. Here's how FDU delivers excellence while meeting your compliance needs:

The Challenge

Finding qualified DVBE contractors who deliver quality

The FDU Solution

NICET IV certified, 24+ years experience, 1000+ successful projects

The Challenge

Navigating complex public procurement requirements

The FDU Solution

DVBE certified, California C-10 licensed, familiar with public works processes

The Challenge

Ensuring compliance with state safety regulations

The FDU Solution

Deep NFPA expertise, current code knowledge, full compliance documentation

Public Sector Project Process

Streamlined procurement compliance from qualification to project closeout.

01

Compliance & Qualification Review

We verify DVBE status, licensing, bonding capacity, and ensure all documentation meets your procurement requirements.

02

Detailed Proposal Submission

Comprehensive proposals with transparent pricing, project timelines, and full technical specifications.

03

Contract Award & Onboarding

Streamlined contract execution with clear milestones, reporting requirements, and communication protocols.

04

Project Delivery & Documentation

On-time delivery with complete as-built documentation, testing reports, and compliance certifications.

Public Sector Project

Regional School District Modernization

Multi-phase fire alarm upgrade across 8 school facilities serving 5,000+ students. Required DVBE participation, strict compliance with state safety standards, and phased installation to minimize disruption during school sessions.

8
Schools
18 Mo
Timeline
100%
Compliance

Frequently Asked Questions

Fire Detection Unlimited is a certified Disabled Veteran Business Enterprise (DVBE). Our veteran-owned status meets California public sector diversity spend requirements while delivering the technical expertise your projects demand.

Ready to Partner with a DVBE Certified Contractor?

Free consultation and detailed proposal within 48 hours. Let's discuss your public sector project needs.
